_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to deliver structured spoken texts for particular purposes and audiences, adjusting for formal and informal contexts and using effective vocal and visual features.

_source.w: We are learning to deliver structured spoken texts for particular purposes and audiences, adjusting for formal and informal contexts and using effective vocal and visual features.
y: Versatile presentation skills — able to shift between formal and informal registers — are highly valued in all areas of life.
Adapting to context and audience demonstrates communicative maturity and audience awareness.
Strong spoken presentation skills build confidence for future academic, professional and civic speaking.
eg: We deliver a formal presentation using appropriate register, structure and vocal features.
We adapt a presentation for different audiences — more formal for a panel, more casual for peers.
We use visual elements like slides, diagrams or props to enhance our spoken message.
We employ features of voice — tone, pace, emphasis, pause — to maintain audience engagement.
We respond to audience questions and feedback effectively.
We evaluate presentations against criteria for purpose, audience, structure and delivery.
